review_title: Leading tool for ecommerce companies
author: Eva G.
pros: Prestahsop offers a great combination of inventory, order and ecommerce management. It´s more expensive than similar tools and offers a good price-quality ratio.
cons: The catalog management becomes quite manual, it would be great to improve the product entry process.

review_title: A Open Source eCommerce platform with free native options for multilingual management to sell internationally
description: A very complete basic solution for setting up your own international online store without having to rely on monthly SAS (Software as a Service) fees. It natively requires additional modules to handle content marketing at the level offered by other CMSs, for example, or to integrate additional functionalities, although there is a PrestaShop Addons Marketplace with modules/themes and a good online community of resources.
author: Jose A R.
pros: Open Source eCommerce platform with free native options for multilingual management and advanced logistics/tax management to sell online internationally in multiple languages ​​in different countries without the need for additional modules
cons: The Open Source version requires initial installation/configuration and ongoing updates, making it a more technical process with significant compatibility issues with older versions, modules, and themes. However, they now also offer a PS Cloud (Hosted/Enterprise) version as a SaaS service, where they handle all the technical aspects for a fee.

review_title: Golden Burrito
author: Damian G.
pros: Solid software, easy shop setup and multi-currency support with a very intuitive administrator dashboard.
cons: I dont like that most of integration which will be needed is just another module mostly paid, which increase starting cost a lot.

review_title: Great e-commerce platform, particularly suitable for large catalogues
description: I have been using PrestaShop for several years, since version 1.6, and I can say that I know it quite thoroughly. I definitely prefer it to other e-commerce platforms for its completeness and the ability to manage large catalogues, even if it occasionally presents some small bugs that are almost never resolved in subsequent releases. Being very widespread, you can find online many third-party modules and templates at low prices.
author: Stefano C.
pros: One of the most complete and efficient e-commerce systems around, very complete, customizable and also suitable for large catalogues. Excellent organization of the backoffice in managing orders and products.
cons: Unfortunately some small but annoying technical bugs are also retained in new releases. It must be known thoroughly in order to make technical changes. Some third-party modules occasionally conflict with each other.

review_title: Prestashop Review
description: Good experience using Prestashop, its a great CMS to work on, as adding product info and managing product data is seamless. there are a lot great features built in also
author: Anwar B.
pros: Easy to navigate, easy to add products and manage products and inventory
cons: some Plugins can be costly when trying to setup a feed for example

review_title: A Beautiful User Interface Shopping Cart Software
description: Its a great shopping cart software , it only needs the developer of the shopping cart to make the admin dashboard to be easy to use, then all countries needs to be added by default in their future versions. As for the front end design, its perfect! then
author: Olusegun S.
pros: I so much love its themes because they're so elegant and responsive on all devices. Prestashop also has some nice plugins which are free then i love its product image zoom feature. Then cross-selling products on Prestashop is excellent. Ability to install different languages at the admin dashboard so that the website visitors can translate the store in their respective languages is great.
cons: Prestashop themes are expensive and their customer support doesn't respond on time which isn't good enough. All countries aren't installed in the localization section of the admin dashboard which will hinder those buyers from the excluded countries from completing their transactions. Whenever you have issues at the admin dashboard section, you must have a basic PHP knowledge to fix it

review_title: An easy-to-use platform for creating simple websites
description: For your online store, Prestashop is analogous to a cargo ship. If you run a small business, you'll appreciate the ease of use and robust features that Prestashop offers for your online store.
author: Stephanie J.
pros: The front-end experience, including the interface, search functionality, and purchasing wizard, must be intuitive and simple to use. Managing products in the administration area is similarly simple and straightforward. The process is simple enough for a person with no technological background to manage.
cons: Theme support is weak when compared to rivals like Shopify and woocommerce. Further, it employs a PHP-based server, making it more vulnerable to malicious software.

review_title: Good, but falling behind WooCommerce for WordPress
description: Nowadays the cons overweight's the pros when we compare with WooCommerce, so we basically almost gave up using it and are fully focused on WordPress, with great ROI based on that bet.
author: Pedro L.
pros: We used to work with Prestashop for ecommerce up until 8 years ago roughly. We started to notice that WooCommerce was starting to evolve and the seamless integration with WordPress is behond "just another plugin". It literally works perfectly together to act as a great ecommerce platform. Also, having experience developing for both platforms (we develop and sell plugins/modules for both platforms) it's a lot faster to develop for WordPress/WooCommerce than it is for Prestashop.
cons: Community is not that huge compared to WordPress. Development, due to the way the core was created, is more time consuming than WordPress and that makes it less appealing because it costs more for customers.

review_title: prestashop pour les ecommerce
author: Samba N.
pros: PrestaShop is an e-commerce CMS similar to WooCommerce that allows you to create online stores easily. The user interface is simple, and there are specific modules available for different types of projects. Templates are easy to find and install, and the platform handles everything from creating to publishing articles or online shops. It’s also simple to integrate an online payment system through APIs from payment service providers.
cons: PrestaShop is quite heavy to install locally and takes up a lot of space. Its modules are paid and often expensive for individuals. There are occasional bugs that can be difficult to fix, and the backend isn’t really designed for non-technical users.

review_title: A good opensource ecommerce platform - but there are better options
author: Christopher D.
pros: I only have experience of prestashop's open-source project, but on the projects I've used it on (a few years ago now) the platform catered for typical ecommerce needs well, allowing a low-cost shop to accept online sales and payments with a simple to use backend for clients.
cons: It was a few years ago that we used Prestashop, we found it to be a bit buggy at times and experienced a fair few breaking changes for updates. For this reason we ended up switching to other systems.

review_title: Great ecommerce system
description: My overall impressions are great. Prestashop is complete as a B2C ecommerce system.
author: Janusz H.
pros: Easy to set up and complete as an ecommerce system
cons: Completely useless b2b mode. I do not know what function it is supposed to have there

review_title: Ideal for companies of any size that want to venture into ecommerce
description: I started using Prestashop as a Joomla or Wordpress plugin and it was a turning point in starting my ecommerce business. It helped me boost my sales and grow my business.
author: Luis Hernan V.
pros: Prestashop is easy to use, it does not require great knowledge to install. You can start with free templates and as a basic user you have great options.For users with more knowledge in Web development, it is a good starting point to avoid starting from 0.It can be customized a lot and the functions it provides are one of the most complete.
cons: The free templates are identical for the most part.

review_title: Make a complete and scalable e-commerce site from the start
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's public domain software and it has a lot of documentation to learn about the developement
cons: Sometimes it's a little bit expensive to implement some solutions and personalizations

review_title: PrestaShop
description: A good choice open source website development platform for developing of PHP based sites. Support for MySql is superb and very easy interface to configure and manage.
author: Muhammad Adil M.
pros: Super easy to install with no glitches. Customisation is easy and lots of options for payment s, international shipping and secure payment. SEO option is helpful and email automation is configurable easily.
cons: If you want to add any module that is very expensive. Design is not that professional and there are issues in scalability.

review_title: Good to start a small business
description: It was great to start, but if you business grows (more references) you can have severe issues
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Prestashop is nice to start a small business, it's easy to use and can connect to different plugins
cons: It's works with plugin : like all solutions that works with plugin their can be dangerous side effects when you add a plugin to your website, and it can create severe performance issues

review_title: In my opinion the best open source platform for online stores
author: Lars F.
pros: Easy to configure, easy to update ( one click update ), easy to install new modules and themes and builders. One of the most versatile platforms for physical and virtual goods. I have custom made for customers shops with upwards of 1 million products, imported from dropshipping suppliers. It is very easy to manage, tons of free and premium modules and upgrades. A solid infrastructure to rely on and different profiles with permission for employs. Very Reliable for large amounts of traffic.
cons: Not that great for backups. Modules and themes don't work from 1.5 to 1.6 to 1.7, have to reorder or to download new version ( incompatible by design of the code ). Big community but not that active. Translations needs an upgrade, being made by contributions from authors.

review_title: Prestashop your simple open source ecommerce software
description: We have worked with Prestashop for clients. It is quite simple to get up and running but the running costs escalate as the project gets bigger. Hosting is a big requirement and you will need plugins for integration as integration can sometimes be a problem when you need to integrate with other software.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Prestashop is free opensource software and a great platform to start off your website. With minimal knowledge you can be up and running. It is not heavily reliant on plugins so you can get your website going without having to purchase plugins. The admin panel is also very user friendly and our clients love it.
cons: Prestashop is not a small platform. You need a lot of hosting space. Talk to an experienced hosting provider to make sure you have the correct hosting package.

review_title: A medium level platform, hard to scale in an enterprise environment.
description: I'm working as a Team Leader in a PrestaShop gold partner Agency, I worked on many PrestaShop projects with success, I spent too many time fixing issues, especially in high traffic projects.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Costs of course, huge marketplace with lots of themes and modules.
cons: Too many versions/releases make this platform often instable, Keep everything updated and stable (struggling with conflicts and incompatibilities) is a great effort for the team.

review_title: Good option for your e-commerce
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: -Easy to deploy. - Big community. -Great plugins.
cons: -Some limitation for bigger e-commerce .

review_title: Great software to use for your online store
author: Gabriel G.
pros: It is one of the most undervalued softwares in the online space. You can really build your store in just a couple of minutes, with tons of modules and themes also from third parties you can have any type of store, either physical or virtual goods. With over 1 million websites constructed with this platform is a great asset to build with.
cons: The difference from major versions is amazing, you have to carefully upgrade on a test website, to not ruin your website. It is best done by a professional

review_title: Great Open-Source Product
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We are using Prestashop for creating the most of our E-commerce products. Open-Source Platform and existing market with plugins - are the main advantages to use Prestashop for small-middle sized projects.
cons: After updating the version of Prestashop, some of the plugins have to be recorded because the system is alerting us that there are some errors in platform update. In case you have a lot of project on Prestashop at the same time, it consumes quite a lot of time.

review_title: Working with PrestaShop as a developer and as an e-tailor
description: We like working with PrestaShop, from both ways we are working with the software. So as well from a developer’s vision as from an e-tailor point of view.
author: Koen D.
pros: As an e-tailor we like the flexibility of PrestaShop as an CMS, the large community who work with the software and the way you can make use of the software. As a developer and PrestaShop Partner we like the diversity of the product and the way we can build converting web shops for our customers.
cons: The one thing we like the least is the assistance we get from PrestaShop themselves. Luckily the community is very active, and this solves most of our problems, but PrestaShop should have a more hands-on mentality.

review_title: Decent Online Store Option, But Has Drawbacks
description: I've got a ton of experience with running and configuring Magento, but I wanted to try PrestaShop simply to use something different on my own business storefront to expand my own knowledge, not to mention that Magento needs more power than PrestaShop. Although PS is easy enough to setup and get running, it lacks a few features which I would consider "standard" by today's terms, not to mention that there is also a serious lack of free/opens source plug-ins available for it like Magento has, which includes things like themes. Overall, the store does its job and works well once all the modules are in place, but In the grand scheme, I would consider PrestaShop as a stepping stone to something bigger and better like Magento or more.
author: Scott F.
pros: Prestashop is for the large part, free to use, and pretty easy to setup and maintain. Most website hosting companies have a single-click installer for it, making getting started easy. Creating categories, products and advanced products is all very easy with very nice menus and layouts, and the default theme is surprisingly useful. Prestashop also comes with many of today's standard features built-in.
cons: The cost of the add-on modules for PrestaShop is ridiculous, and so is the platform's reliance on those modules. There is no way to do anything except free or basic flat-rate shipping unless you spend considerable monies on buying realtime rates modules for EACH shipper, or subscribing to a third-party app that combines and does the checking in a single package, which is costly. Accessing anything except the software's basic features can be incredibly clumsy as well.

review_title: it a great program to star an e-commerce business
description: professional tools because it's a very common tool that every e-commerce business use.
author: Alfredo R.
pros: It's an easy to use program, friendly UX, responsive, it covers all the basic needs for a basic e-commerce business, great library of admins to upgrade your business
cons: It's slow, you have to save your progress several times because not always saves what you do, not that prewar web templates.

review_title: One of the best user-friendly free shopping cart software
description: I've started using the PrestaShop around 2 years ago and I'm still into this system. The experience with the system I got during all this time and throughout my several projects is enough to write ah honest review about the system. Starting with the advantages, the biggest advantage of the PrestaShop is its huge support community which is the best ever help a developer can dream of. Discussion boards with active users make a big difference. Furthermore, one of the biggest advantages is also the administration panel of the PrestaShop which is way easy to use than say other competitor platforms. What's more important for me is the extended functionality and efficiency. The function ility is far more than any user would ever think to want to have on their websites and as for efficiency, PrestaShop is lightweight and well optimised, thus not loading the servers. As for the disadvantages, there are of course some. The biggest disadvantage is that most of the most useful functionalities require the additional investments to have installed in my PrestaShop. What I also noticed, in a case of big products quantity, Prestashop does not function as well as it initially did. To conclude, PrestaShop is good for starting the e-commerce. The system works well with medium or a small number of products, but PrestaShop's efficiency, when there are 100 products isn't any better than when there are 1000. Overall, I would suggest this to the beginner in e-commerce users.
author: Giorgi G.
